business.headline: Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

Live: Mourners chant 'Death to America' at Ali Khamenei's funeralUS-Iran Conflict Live Updates: Thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran on Saturday as Iran commenced the funeral ceremony for its late Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with several foreign leaders and senior officials attending one of the country's most significant state events in decades. The first day of the programme at Tehran's Grand Mosalla complex drew high-ranking delegations from across the Middle East and beyond, including heads of state, government representatives and senior diplomats, underscoring Khamenei's influence across the region. Among those who paid their respects were senior officials from allied countries, while messages of condolence continued to pour in from governments around the world. Iran's top political and military leadership was present for the ceremony, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi. Emotional scenes unfolded during the proceedings as Ghalibaf and Araghchi were seen breaking down in tears while paying their final respects to the late Supreme Leader. The ceremony was held under tight security, with authorities imposing extensive traffic restrictions across the capital as thousands of mourners streamed into the Grand Mosalla complex. Many walked several kilometres to reach the venue, while long queues formed outside metro stations from the early hours of the morning. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

sports.headline: England bank on secret weapon ahead of Round of 16 match vs Mexico: Viagra England could legally use Viagra to help cope with the high altitude in Mexico City ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash against co-hosts Mexico.
